articles.title: Delhi LG reviews heatwave relief with cooling zones across eight districts - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Delhi Lieutenant Governor Taranjit Singh Sandhu reviewed relief operations and preventive measures addressing the ongoing heatwave in the national capital on Thursday evening. The Divisional Commissioner and other concerned officers attended the meeting, according to a press note.
The LG was informed that the Revenue Department, GNCTD, had established several dedicated Cooling Zones across eight districts at various locations. Mobile Heat Relief Units (MHRU) have been deployed since May 6, 2026, actively providing relief and essential support to citizens in all 13 districts, officials said.
On Tuesday, the Delhi government intensified preparations for the heatwave, with Health Minister Dr Pankaj Kumar Singh inspecting arrangements at Lok Nayak Jai Prakash (LNJP) Hospital. During his visit, he examined emergency care systems, ICU facilities, medical staff readiness, and patient management infrastructure.
Dr Singh stated that the health department and medical teams continuously monitor the situation to prevent any inconvenience due to extreme heat. He appealed to residents to avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight and to seek immediate medical attention if experiencing symptoms such as weakness, dizziness, dehydration, fever, or heat-related illness.
He concluded, “Delhi’s healthcare system is fully prepared to deal with every challenge posed by the heatwave. Our priority is to ensure that no citizen faces any difficulty during this period of extreme heat.”
To protect personnel working under severe conditions, the department launched a high-tech relief initiative, introducing air-conditioned helmets and portable fans for officers managing busy intersections. This cooling headgear is being piloted for traffic personnel stationed for extended periods in direct sunlight.
Assistant Sub-Inspector Veer Singh described the technology as “a game-changer. The helmets keep officers cool and relaxed throughout their shift.” He said, “This helmet keeps us relaxed in this weather. It can work easily for 4-5 hours, depending on the charge in its battery…”
The cooling system operates effectively for 4-5 hours, providing sustained comfort during peak heat. For those overseeing VIP movements, mobile vans have been deployed as hydration and nutrition hubs to supply essential water and food, preventing exhaustion.
The initiative coincides with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing Red and Orange alerts across multiple states, signalling a period of severe, life-threatening temperatures.

articles.title: Rain To Hit Delhi Today, But When Will Heatwave Return? Check What IMD Says - Times Now
articles.full_text:
Delhi Weather Today: Delhi is expected to see rain on Saturday (June 6) as temperatures continue to drop, providing relief to residents. However, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), this would not last long as the heatwave is just about to return to the national capital. Once again, the temperatures are expected to touch 40 degrees Celsius.
According to the IMD, parts of Delhi are expected to see rain today, with the met department forecasting "thunderstorm and rain". However, no such alert has been issued for Saturday. The maximum temperature is expected to touch 36 degrees Celsius, while the minimum is likely to drop to 25 degrees Celsius.
Strong winds of 40 to 60 kilometers per hour are possible in the afternoon. As per the met department, thunderstorms, lightning, and light rain are likely to occur in Delhi-NCR due to a western disturbance and an active cyclonic system in the plains.
When Will Heatwave Return?
After two to three days of rain and cool weather, Delhi is expected to witness June's scorching heat from next week as once again mercury will touch 40 degrees Celsius, and is expected to increase post that.
The met department has not issued any alerts for next week, but it is expected to change. Moreover, the IMD has predicted "strong surface winds during daytime" next week.
Earlier on Friday, Delhi was placed under "yellow alert", however, only a few areas in the national capital were hit by light rain. The temperature dropped to 25 degrees last night, with a maximum of 36 degrees Celsius.
"Generally cloudy sky. A spell of light rain accompanied by thunderstorms/lightning and strong winds reaching 40–50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph, during the morning to forenoon. Another spell of very light rain accompanied by thunderstorms/lightning is likely during the evening/night," the IMD forecasted for Friday.

articles.title: Delhi Weather Today: दिल्ली में गरज-चमक के साथ बारिश के आसार, लेकिन क्या जल्द लौटेगी लू? जानें IMD का वेदर फोरकास्ट - Moneycontrol Hindi
articles.full_text:
Delhi Weather Today: दिल्ली में आज यानी 6 जून को तापमान में गिरावट जारी रहने के कारण बारिश होने की संभावना है। जिससे लोगों को उमस भरी गर्मी से राहत मिलेगी। हालांकि, भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) के अनुसार, यह राहत ज्यादा देर तक नहीं रहेगी क्योंकि राष्ट्रीय राजधानी में लू फिर से लौटने वाली है। तापमान एक बार फिर 40 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंचने की उम्मीद है।
IMD के मुताबिक, दिल्ली के कुछ हिस्सों में आज गरज के साथ बारिश हो सकती है। इस दौरान अधिकतम तापमान करीब 36 डिग्री और न्यूनतम तापमान लगभग 25 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहने की उम्मीद है।
वहीं, दोपहर के समय 40 से 60 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, पश्चिमी विक्षोभ और मैदानी इलाकों में सक्रिय चक्रवाती सिस्टम की वजह से दिल्ली-एनसीआर में गरज-चमक के साथ आंधी, बिजली गिरने और हल्की बारिश होने की संभावना है।
दो-तीन दिनों की बारिश और ठंडे मौसम के बाद, अगले हफ्ते से दिल्ली में जून की तेज गर्मी वापस लौटने की संभावना है, क्योंकि पारा एक बार फिर 40 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच जाएगा और उसके बाद इसमें और बढ़ोतरी होने की उम्मीद है।
वहीं, मौसम विभाग ने अगले हफ्ते के लिए कोई अलर्ट जारी नहीं किया है, लेकिन इसमें बदलाव की संभावना है। इसके अलावा, IMD ने अगले सप्ताह "दिन के समय तेज सतही हवाओं" का पूर्वानुमान लगाया है।
मौसम विभाग ने आने वाले शुक्रवार के लिए पूर्वानुमान जारी किया है, "आसमान में आमतौर पर बादल छाए रहेंगे। सुबह से दोपहर तक हल्की बारिश के साथ गरज, बिजली कड़कने और 40-50 किमी प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चलने की संभावना है, जिनकी रफ्तार 60 किमी प्रति घंटे तक पहुंच सकती है। शाम या रात के दौरान गरज-चमक के साथ हल्की बारिश का एक और दौर देखने को मिल सकता है।"
